# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

interface ModuleConfig {
  path: string;
  name: string;
}

interface PluginConfig {
  modules: ModuleConfig[];
}

const config: PluginConfig = {
  modules: [],
};

async function loadModule(modulePath: string): Promise<any> {
  try {
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    return module;
  } catch (error) {
    console.error(`Failed to load module ${modulePath}:`, error);
    throw error;
  }
}

function parseConfigFromEnv(): PluginConfig {
  const modulesStr = Deno.env.get("MODULES");
  if (!modulesStr) return config;
  
  try {
    const modules = JSON.parse(modulesStr);
    return { modules };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Invalid MODULES environment variable:", error);
    return config;
  }
}

function parseConfigFromQuery(url: string): PluginConfig {
  const parsedUrl = new URL(url);
  const modulesStr = parsedUrl.searchParams.get("modules");
  
  if (!modulesStr) return config;
  
  try {
    const modules = JSON.parse(modulesStr);
    return { modules };
  } catch (error) {
    console.error("Invalid modules query parameter:", error);
    return config;
  }
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);
  const configFromEnv = parseConfigFromEnv();
  const configFromQuery = parseConfigFromQuery(request.url);
  
  const finalConfig = {
    modules: [...configFromEnv.modules, ...configFromQuery.modules]
  };
  
  if (finalConfig.modules.length === 0) {
    return new Response("No modules configured", { status: 400 });
  }
  
  const loadedModules: any[] = [];
  
  for (const moduleConfig of finalConfig.modules) {
    try {
      const module = await loadModule(moduleConfig.path);
      loadedModules.push({ name: moduleConfig.name, module });
    } catch (error) {
      console.error(`Failed to load module ${moduleConfig.name}:`, error);
    }
  }
  
  return new Response(
    JSON.stringify({
      message: "Modules loaded successfully",
      loadedModules: loadedModules.map(m => m.name),
    }),
    { headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" } }
  );
}

Deno.serve(handleRequest);
